description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, elevated on a single sturdy pole anchored into a concrete base set in a circular patch of grass. The array consists of twelve rectangular photovoltaic modules arranged in a 3x4 grid, each with a visible grid of blue-black solar cells and metallic framing. The panel surface is angled upward, likely to optimize sunlight capture, and the supporting pole is made of metal, providing stability and height. The mounting structure appears robust, with a metallic frame supporting the solar modules and a concrete block at the base for secure installation. No visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is present on the exterior. The overall design is typical for small-scale solar installations in parks, open urban spaces, or near public amenities.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a metal pole with a concrete base, featuring twelve blue-black photovoltaic modules in a 3x4 grid, set on a grassy patch.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the metallic frame, pole, and concrete base supporting the angled solar panel array.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panel array and the single supporting pole anchored in concrete.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full 3x4 grid of blue-black solar modules and the supporting structure beneath.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled profile of the solar array and the mounting pole.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing away from the mounting pole, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a steel or aluminum pole anchored in a concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid of solar panels arranged in a single rectangular array
